notes: Ayton cleared in a strong interior role game
Lakers frontcourt held enough rebound share despite Luka-led scoring dominance
This leg aligned with pregame role and environment read
Jarrett Allen remained productive and Thomas Bryant added 25 bench minutes with 6 rebounds
Cleveland frontcourt rebound share fragmented more than expected
Game script and reduced Mobley minutes broke the over despite favorable pregame environment

model_takeaway: Ayton validation confirms Lakers-side big rebound thesis was sound
Mobley miss was driven more by minutes suppression and internal share dilution than total environment
Need stronger penalty when a target big can lose volume to another starting center plus active bench big minutes

postmortem_summary.key_failure_point: Model overweighted Mobley's centralized rebound ownership under a dual-big plus Bryant bench-minute environment
postmortem_summary.action_item: Increase downgrade when another center is active and rebound-capable bench big projects enough minutes to siphon glass share
